messages.content: WASI keeps coming up. What is it and when would I use it over Docker containers?

WASI (WebAssembly System Interface) lets WASM run outside the browser:

**WASI gives WebAssembly**:
- File system access (sandboxed)
- Network access (capability-based)
- Environment variables
- Standard I/O

**WASI vs Docker containers**:
- **Startup time**: WASI ~1ms vs Docker ~100ms-1s
- **Size**: WASI module ~1-10MB vs Docker image ~100MB-1GB
- **Security**: WASI is sandboxed by default (capability-based). Docker needs careful configuration.
- **Portability**: WASI runs on any WASI runtime (Wasmtime, Wasmer, WasmEdge). No Linux dependency.

**When WASI makes sense**:
- Serverless functions (sub-millisecond cold starts)
- Plugin systems (run user code safely)
- Edge computing (tiny footprint)
- Portable CLI tools

**When Docker is better**:
- Complex applications with many dependencies
- Existing container ecosystem (Kubernetes, registries)
- Need full OS capabilities
- Your language doesn't compile to WASM well

The famous quote: "If WASM+WASI existed in 2008, we wouldn't have needed to create Docker" - Solomon Hykes (Docker co-founder).

WASI is most impactful for: serverless (Fermyon Spin, Cloudflare Workers) and plugin systems (Envoy proxy filters, database extensions).
